title: Maps and Sets (ES6)
content:
### Maps and Sets

ES6 introduced **Map** and **Set** -- new collection types beyond objects and arrays.

---

### Map -- Key-Value Pairs

A **Map** holds key-value pairs where keys can be **any type** (not just strings).

```js
const map = new Map();

map.set("name", "John");
map.set(42, "The answer");
map.set(true, "boolean key");

console.log(map.get("name"));  // "John"
console.log(map.has(42));       // true
console.log(map.size);          // 3
map.delete("name");
map.clear();                    // Remove all
```

#### Map vs Object

| Feature | Object | Map |
|---------|--------|-----|
| **Key type** | String/Symbol only | Any type |
| **Order** | Not guaranteed (old) | Insertion order |
| **Size** | Manual count | `.size` property |
| **Iteration** | `for...in`, `Object.keys()` | `forEach()`, `for...of` |
| **Performance** | Good | Better for frequent add/remove |

---

### Set -- Unique Values

A **Set** stores **unique** values of any type. Duplicates are automatically removed.

```js
const set = new Set();

set.add(1);
set.add(2);
set.add(2); // Ignored (duplicate)
set.add(3);

console.log(set.size);  // 3
console.log(set.has(2)); // true
set.delete(1);
```

#### Practical: Remove Duplicates from Array

```js
const numbers = [1, 2, 2, 3, 3, 3, 4];
const unique = [...new Set(numbers)];
console.log(unique); // [1, 2, 3, 4]
```

---

### WeakMap and WeakSet

**WeakMap** and **WeakSet** are special versions where keys/values are **weakly referenced**:

- Keys can be **garbage collected** if no other references exist
- No `.size`, no `.forEach()`, no `.clear()`
- Used for **memory-sensitive** caches and metadata

```js
const weakMap = new WeakMap();
let obj = { data: "secret" };
weakMap.set(obj, "metadata");
obj = null; // Entry in weakMap can now be garbage collected
```

> **When to use what:** Use `Map` for general key-value storage with non-string keys. Use `Set` when you need uniqueness. Use `WeakMap`/`WeakSet` for memory-sensitive caching.

quiz.question: What happens if you add a duplicate value to a Set?
